title: actlint standards & regulatory crosswalk
description: Maps each stable RuleId to the external frameworks it is relevant to (OWASP ASI, OWASP MCP Top 10, CoSAI/OASIS, EU AI Act, NIST) and the MCP annotation field it concerns. Pure data: it carries no mechanism. Standards references are relevant, not prescriptive — a finding is never a compliance verdict. Every RuleId the engine can emit must have a non-empty entry here; that completeness is a contract test in packages/core.

version.description: Semver of the crosswalk dataset itself — an independent line from the engine and vocabulary versions.

note.description: The 'relevant, not prescriptive' caveat, stated in the data so it travels with it.

map.description: RuleId -> the standards it is relevant to. Keys are stable, kebab-case RuleIds.

standardsRef.description: At least one array must be present and non-empty — a finding that cannot locate itself in the standards landscape is unshippable.
